body.en: Structural phase fasteners are load-bearing and critical to building safety: Anchor bolts (J-bolts, L-bolts) — M20/M24 for column base plates; embed in concrete during pour; specify HDG or 316 SS for coastal; quantity = 4 per column minimum. Structural hex bolts — M16/M20 Grade 10.9 for beam-to-column, beam-to-beam connections; specify HDG for outdoor exposed; quantity = per connection design (typically 4-8 per splice). Base plate shims — thin steel plates for leveling steel columns before grouting; size to match base plate. Shear studs — M19×100 Nelson studs for composite steel-deck floors; welded to beam flanges before deck installation. High-strength F1554 Grade 55 or 105 for anchor bolts in seismic zones. This procurement framework applies to fastener sourcing for construction, mining, energy, automotive, and general industrial applications. The decision logic prioritizes quality consistency, supplier reliability, and total cost of ownership over short-term unit price. For B2B buyers in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia, additional considerations include regional customs requirements, container consolidation options, and payment security mechanisms. Standardized specifications that travel across projects reduce training time, RFQ processing time, and supplier onboarding effort. body.en: Roofing fasteners must resist wind uplift and provide weather sealing: IBR/Trapezoidal roofing screws — #3 drill point, hex head with EPDM washer; 6mm (for 0.5mm sheet) or 8mm (for 0.8mm sheet) drill point; quantity = 4-6 per m² for roofs, 6-8 per m² for walls; always calculate based on wind zone. Side-lap screws — #2 drill point, pan head, 50mm long; one per lap rib at 500mm intervals. Ridge cap screws — same as roofing screws; install at both sides of ridge. Flashing nails — ring shank nails for securing flashing; hot-dip galvanized. For coastal areas (within 10km of ocean), specify stainless steel 304 roofing screws. For color-matched sheeting, specify polymer-coated in the correct RAL color — typically 500+ hours salt spray resistance.  Standards-based procurement requires continuous learning as ISO, ASTM, DIN, and GB standards are revised every 3-5 years. Subscribe to standards update notifications and review specifications annually. The most common standards used in international fastener trade are ISO 898-1 (mechanical properties), ISO 3506-1 (stainless), ISO 3269 (acceptance inspection), ISO 4042 (coatings), and ISO 1502 (thread gauges). For B2B buyers, building a standards reference library with at least the top 20 standards relevant to your product mix is essential. body.fr: Roofing fasteners must resist wind uplift and provide weather sealing: IBR/Trapezoidal roofing screws — #3 drill point, hex head with EPDM washer; 6mm (for 0.5mm sheet) or 8mm (for 0.8mm sheet) drill point; quantity = 4-6 per m² for roofs, 6-8 per m² for walls; always calculate based on wind zone. Side-lap screws — #2 drill point, pan head, 50mm long; one per lap rib at 500mm intervals. Ridge cap screws — same as roofing screws; install at both sides of ridge. Ceiling channel (purlin) screws — #3 drill point, hex head, for ceiling grid to structural steel; M6×25mm for suspended ceiling Main Tees. Partition studs — No. 8 × 90mm bugle head screws for stud-to-track connections. Gyproc cornices —掰Gyproc screws for cornice fixing. Acoustic insulation clips — resilient channels for sound-rated partitions. For humid African environments (coastal, swimming pool buildings), use zinc-plated or stainless steel drywall screws to prevent corrosion staining. body.en: Finishing phase fasteners — visible and often color-matched: Door and window frame screws — countersunk head, 100mm length for door frames to masonry/timber; specify stainless steel for coastal. Hinge screws — 30mm stainless steel screws for door hinges (replacing standard slotted with security head). Handrail brackets — M8 stainless steel screws for wall-mounting handrails. Sanitary ware — concealed fixing screws for toilets, basins, and bidets; stainless steel with white caps. Mirror fixings — special mirror clips with rubber buffer; stainless steel for humid bathrooms. Cabinet hinges — 15mm screws (provided with hinges usually). Skirting board nails — lost-head nails (brads) 50mm for timber skirtings; or grab screws for adhesive-fixed skirtings. body.en: For estimating fastener quantities at construction planning stage: Rule of thumb — allow 25-40kg of mixed fasteners per 100m² of building (excluding structural steel). For roofing — allow 6-8 screws per m² of IBR roof sheeting (depending on wind zone); for cladding allow 8-10 per m². For drywall — allow approximately 1 kg of drywall screws per 10m² of drywall. For electrical — allow approximately 1 bag (500g) of wall plugs per 50m²; plus 1 box of cable clips per 100m² of building. These are estimates only — always verify against actual project specifications and structural engineer's requirements.  For B2B buyers operating in multiple countries, harmonize specifications with regional standards and local customs requirements. Common regional frameworks: SABS (South Africa), KEBS (Kenya), TBS (Tanzania), ZIMRA (Zimbabwe), SON (Nigeria), and AfCFTA standards for pan-Africa trade. Local standards compliance is often mandatory for public infrastructure projects, mining operations, and government tenders.
